title: Permission Incorrectly Described
excerpt: The way this permission is described would be perfect, but for some reason people can still edit and delete views and automations, which literally changes the structure of the board. Not sure why this is. Am I missing…

posts.author: Lucas2 (Lucas Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Screenshot 2023-10-09 at 12.47.42 PM2392×692 64.7 KB

The way this permission is described would be perfect, but for some reason people can still edit and delete views and automations, which literally changes the structure of the board. Not sure why this is. Am I missing something? I had these permissions set and had a normal member, not admin or board owner, delete a view. This is changing the structure of the board and should not be allowed

posts.author: Lucas2 (Lucas Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml: More specifically DELETING views, which is absolutely changing the structure of a board. We started letting our employees use our Monday.com only a week ago, and they are already deleting views on accident haha.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Lock views (default views for all users and hide main table) Feature requests
Hi Community! Thank you for your feedback! i have an exciting update. We have developed and released the ability to lock views and are working on the private views feature which is on our long-term roadmap and estimated to be available in 2024 ( subject to change). Thank you for being part of our development process and keep sharing your ideas with us!

However, I have found not all accounts are showing the lock view option. Reaching out to support@monday.com may be needed to find out why its not turned on in your account.

Unfortunately, you are correct though that the permission you found doesn’t prevent changing views. Its been a long term sore point, definitely not new (as you see by the above).
